claimText: The Foreign Currency Settlement System (FCSS) at GIFT IFSC is authorized by IFSCA under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
evidenceExcerpt: It is authorized by IFSCA under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007, to settle foreign currency transactions locally.
examUse: Statement-based question on the authorizing body and legislative backing of the FCSS.
distractorHooks: Authorized by RBI instead of IFSCA
Authorized under FEMA instead of Payment and Settlement Systems Act

claimText: CCIL IFSC Limited is the operator of the Foreign Currency Settlement System (FCSS) at GIFT IFSC.
evidenceExcerpt: CCIL IFSC Limited is the operator of the FCSS.
examUse: Fact-based or correct-pair question identifying the operator of the FCSS.

claimText: GIFT City is India's first and only IFSC and is designated as a non-resident zone under FEMA.
evidenceExcerpt: GIFT City is India's first and only IFSC, established as an SEZ in 2015 and designated as a non-resident zone under FEMA.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal and residency status of GIFT City.
distractorHooks: Regulated solely under the SEZ Act without FEMA non-resident status

claimText: The International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) was established in 2020 under the IFSCA Act, 2019 to regulate financial products and institutions in IFSCs.
evidenceExcerpt: IFSCA was established in 2020 under the IFSCA Act, 2019 to regulate financial products and institutions in IFSCs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the regulatory jurisdiction over IFSCs.
distractorHooks: Established in 2015 along with GIFT City
